name: TurnOnGreen, Inc.
business: TurnOnGreen designs, develops, manufactures, and sells highly engineered power conversion systems and EV charging solutions. It serves defense, aerospace, medical, industrial, telecommunications, e-Mobility, and OEM markets. The company also offers EV charging infrastructure and subscription-based network management services.

exposures.sentence: We offer a broad range of rugged power solutions for the defense and aerospace market.

Revenues from the medical and healthcare industry accounted for approximately 3% and 11% of all revenues received from our power supply products for the years ended December 31, 2025, and 2024, respectively.

Our industrial power solutions are designed to stand up to the extreme temperatures, input surges, vibration and shock found through uses such as industrial automation, material handling, industrial lasers, robotics, agriculture, oil, and gas, mining and outdoor applications.

In the industrial and telecommunications industry, typical applications include packaging equipment, laboratory and diagnostic equipment, industrial laser drivers, data center computing infrastructure, and turbomachinery control solutions.

Approximately 17% of TurnOnGreen s revenue is generated leveraging its core power technologies to deliver comprehensive EV charging infrastructure and subscription-based charging network management services for residential, fleet, hospitality, workplace, healthcare, municipal, and educational environments including universities and schools.

Digital Power offers high-performance and high-power data-center power supplies, semiconductor fabrication equipment power source supplies, desktop power supplies, and a comprehensive range of value-added customized AC/DC and DC/DC ruggedized power supply and system solutions.
For more than 50 years, Digital Power has provided rugged products and custom power solutions designed end-to-end for military and aerospace applications.

While the global chip shortage has eased, certain high-demand components (such as power semiconductors and automotive semiconductor chips), continue to face supply bottlenecks due to limited fab capacity and ongoing geopolitical factors.

The costs of key inputs, including copper, transformers, capacitors, and other passive components, have increased, driven in part by strong demand associated with artificial intelligence infrastructure.
